game_name: Super Star Wars: Return of the Jedi

design_philosophy.core_loop: Side-scrolling action platforming with character-specific mechanics across themed levels from Return of the Jedi, punctuated by Mode 7 vehicle sequences
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Precise traversal and combat execution against enemies and environmental hazards; managing character-specific abilities and loadouts
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Playing as iconic Star Wars characters (Luke, Han, Leia, Chewbacca, Wicket) progressing through film scenes with mechanical variety
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Heavy emphasis on character differentiation—each character has distinct movesets and playstyles; Leia's appearance and abilities shift narratively based on story context (Boushh bounty hunter, escaped slave, Rebel leader); strict level-by-level character assignment limits player choice but reinforces narrative coherence

difficulty.lives_system: Configurable via debug menu; standard respawn on loss
difficulty.continues_system: Password save system allows resuming progress between sessions
difficulty.checkpointing: Stage-based checkpointing; no mid-level saves documented
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Limited; fixed character assignments per level, difficulty determined by level design rather than player-selectable modes. Debug menu (cheat codes) provides invincibility and stage skip for experienced players

progression.upgrade_system: Power-ups and temporary ability enhancements (Luke's Force powers reduced to 5 from 9 in predecessor; rechargeable via gameplay). Character-specific weapons: Leia wields staff, chain, blaster depending on narrative stage; Luke has no blaster option in this iteration
progression.unlock_mechanism: Linear progression through film-based stages; characters unlocked/assigned per level narrative context
replayability_hooks: Five playable characters with mechanically distinct movesets encourage replaying levels with different characters
Vehicle sequences (speeder bike, Millennium Falcon) provide gameplay variety breaks
Password system enables selective stage re-engagement
Post-game access via debug menu for speedrunning or challenge runs

world_building_techniques: Direct film plot adaptation with added scenes (Luke infiltrating Death Star)
Character cosmetic progression: Leia's outfit and ability changes mirror narrative role shifts
Vehicle sequences contextualize key film moments (Endor speeder chase, Millennium Falcon escape)
Boss encounters directly correspond to film antagonists and story beats (Jabba, Rancor, Vader, Emperor)

boss_design.philosophy: Iconic film antagonists adapted as challenging but learnable encounters; bosses emphasize pattern recognition and character-specific counter-strategies

faction_indicators: Rebel Alliance (playable characters: Luke, Han, Leia, Chewbacca, Wicket)
Galactic Empire (antagonistic forces, Emperor as final boss)
Jabba's Criminal Syndicate (Jabba as boss, Leia infiltration sequence)
danger_signals: Imperial troops and stormtroopers as recurring enemies
Rancor beast as enforcer of Jabba's authority
Death Star as seat of Emperor's power; Luke must breach it to confront tyranny
environmental_storytelling: Jabba's Palace: opulent criminal stronghold, Leia must escape as enslaved prisoner
Sail Barge: mobile fortress and execution platform, battle during escape
Endor forests: Rebel sanctuary, speeder bike chase through natural terrain
Death Star: technological monolith of Empire, final confrontation with Vader and Emperor
Millennium Falcon sequences: mobile escape, continuity of Rebel safe haven

sources_used: tcrf (The Cutting Room Floor - Super Star Wars: Return of the Jedi SNES article)
wikipedia (Super Star Wars: Return of the Jedi Wikipedia article)
